Tumor regression 22% at last scan. |\n| CU-002 | 18 | 9.1 | 0.12 | 1.09 | q3w | 2026-04-12 | 2.8 | Responding well. Weight gain improving. |\n| CU-003 | 51 | 15.7 | 0.18 | 2.83 | q2w | 2026-04-10 | 4.1 | New enrollment. First dose administered 2026-03-27. |\n\n## Quick assessment\n- Current dosing parameters are present for all three compassionate-use patients.\n- Patient notes do not indicate any immediate instability in the latest dosing file.\n- Based on the config alone, there are no obvious PK-related dosing anomalies.\n- Full PK model operational stability cannot be confirmed from the config file alone; that would require current runtime/monitoring outputs.\n", recipients="")]Yes — I pulled the latest dosing parameters and drafted a quick summary for Dr. Martinez.

Summary:
- CU-001: 0.15 mg/kg × 12.4 kg = 1.86 mg, q3w, next dose 2026-04-14, clearance 3.2 mL/min
- CU-002: 0.12 mg/kg × 9.1 kg = 1.09 mg, q3w, next dose 2026-04-12, clearance 2.8 mL/min
- CU-003: 0.18 mg/kg × 15.7 kg = 2.83 mg, q2w, next dose 2026-04-10, clearance 4.1 mL/min

Patient notes:
- CU-001: Stable; 22% tumor regression at last scan
- CU-002: Responding well; weight gain improving
- CU-003: Newly enrolled; first dose given 2026-03-27

On PK stability:
- Based on the latest dosing config, nothing stands out as unstable or inconsistent.
- All three patients have defined dosing schedules and PK clearance values.
- That said, this file alone doesn’t confirm live model runtime health — only that the current dosing parameters look coherent.

I’ve saved the report as:
- Project Helios Compassionate-Use Dosing Summary for Dr. Martinez

If you want, I can also format this into a shorter clinician-facing note.
